zh.lifeSummary: 當Barbara Chandler於1553年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Thomas Chandler III26歲，母親Joane Momfort Paige22歲。她於大約1572年與Richard Rogers在英國艾可維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1598年在英國艾可享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: Chandler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a maker and seller of candles, from Anglo-Norman French chandeler ‘maker or seller of candles’ (Old French chandelier, Late Latin candelarius, a derivative of candela ‘candle’). While a medieval chandler no doubt made and sold other articles beside candles, the extended sense of modern English chandler does not occur until the 16th century. The name may also, more rarely, have denoted someone who was responsible for the lighting arrangements in a large house, or else one who owed rent in the form of wax or candles.

variants.name: Candler
meanings: English (East Anglia and southeastern England): variant of   Chandler  .
German: variant of Kandler and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Candler is very rare in Germany.

nameMeanings.name: Barbara
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

variants.name: Barbra
meanings: From Latin, meaning ‘foreign woman’ (a feminine form of barbarus ‘foreign’, from Greek, referring originally to the unintelligible chatter of foreigners, which sounded to the Greek ear like no more than bar-bar). St Barbara has always been one of the most popular saints in the calendar, although there is some doubt whether she ever actually existed. According to legend, she was imprisoned in a tower and later murdered by her father, who was then struck down by a bolt of lightning. Accordingly, she is the patron of architects, stonemasons, and fortifications, and of firework makers, artillerymen, and gunpowder magazines.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Barbara Chandler was born in 1553, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Thomas Chandler III, was 26 and her mother, Joane Momfort Paige, was 22. She married Richard Rogers about 1572, in Wethersfield, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 1598, in Essex, England, at the age of 46.

ko.lifeSummary: Barbara Chandler(이)가 1553년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Chandler III(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joane Momfort Paige(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1572년 영국 에세스 w더스필드에서 Richard Rogers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1598년 영국 에세스에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Barbara Chandler родилась в 1553 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Chandler III было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joane Momfort Paige, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Rogers около 1572 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1598 г. в местности эссекс, Англия в возрасте 46 лет.
